body:
Checksum verification can be silently skipped if FindChecksum doesn't populate $(ExpectedHash) (e.g., checksum file format change or binary name mismatch). Because the <Error> only triggers when ExpectedHash is non-empty, the build may proceed without any integrity check. Consider failing fast when ExpectedHash is empty, and gating the "checksum verified" <Message> on ExpectedHash/ComputedHash being non-empty as well.
```suggestion
    <Error Text="Tailwind CLI checksum not found in checksum file for '$(TailwindBinaryName)'. Aborting to avoid skipping integrity verification." Condition="'$(ExpectedHash)' == ''" />
    <Error Text="Tailwind CLI checksum mismatch! Expected: $(ExpectedHash), Computed: $(ComputedHash)" Condition="'$(ExpectedHash)' != '' and '$(ComputedHash)' != '' and '$(ExpectedHash.ToLower())' != '$(ComputedHash.ToLower())'" />
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Tailwind CLI checksum verified: $(ComputedHash)" Condition="'$(ExpectedHash)' != '' and '$(ComputedHash)' != '' and '$(ExpectedHash.ToLower())' == '$(ComputedHash.ToLower())'" />
```

TailwindLocalPath is built using a hard-coded backslash. On non-Windows builds this can produce an invalid path string and break the download/move/chmod steps. Prefer using forward slashes or MSBuild path-combining properties so the path is correct cross-platform.
```suggestion
    <TailwindLocalPath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)/tailwindcss</TailwindLocalPath>
```

The XML doc says GetTailwindPath returns null when not found, but the implementation always throws FileNotFoundException instead of returning null. Either update the docs to reflect the throwing behavior, or switch to a TryGet... pattern that returns null/false and lets callers decide how to handle missing binaries.

ProcessUtils.ExecuteProcessAsync returns empty Stdout/Stderr when streamOutput=true, so this error log will always print an empty {Stderr}. Either capture stderr while streaming (e.g., buffer lines) or remove the Stderr field from this log message when streamOutput is enabled. Also, GetTailwindPath currently throws on missing binary, so the null-coalescing fallback to "tailwindcss" is unreachable and can be removed once the API behavior is clarified.

This method's XML docs state it throws InvalidOperationException if the process fails to start, but when process.Start() returns false it returns a CommandResult with ExitCode -1 instead. Align the behavior with the docs (throw) or update the docs/return contract so callers can reliably handle startup failures.
```suggestion
                throw new InvalidOperationException($"Failed to start process '{fileName}'.");
```

Unused using: System.Runtime.InteropServices is not referenced in this file and will produce warnings under typical analyzers. Remove it if it's not needed.
```suggestion

```

Process execution is core infrastructure and has several edge cases (argument passing, working directory, cancellation/kill behavior, streaming vs captured output). Consider adding unit tests in ForgeTrust.Runnable.Core.Tests for ExecuteProcessAsync covering success, non-zero exit code output capture, and cancellation/kill behavior to prevent regressions.

Service registration here uses AddSingleton/AddHostedService directly, which can lead to duplicate registrations (and multiple watch services running) if AddTailwind is called more than once. Elsewhere in the repo, extension methods prefer AddOptions + Configure and TryAddSingleton to avoid duplicates (e.g., Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorWire/RazorWireServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:24-33). Consider switching TailwindCliManager registration to TryAddSingleton and adding the hosted service via TryAddEnumerable so repeated calls are idempotent.

This repository-level tailwind.version file doesn't appear to be referenced by the build logic (the project uses $(TailwindVersion) and stamps $(IntermediateOutputPath)tailwind.version instead). Consider either wiring this file into the MSBuild property (to avoid version drift) or removing it if it's not intended to be consumed by tooling.
```suggestion

```

**Restore cache-busting on the generated stylesheet link to align with other layouts.**

Line 12 references `site.gen.css` without `asp-append-version="true"`, causing stale CSS to persist in browser cache after rebuilds. The RazorDocs layout in the same project correctly includes this attribute for the same stylesheetтАФapply the same pattern here.

<details>
<summary>ЁЯТб Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-    <link rel="stylesheet" href="~/css/site.gen.css"/>
+    <link rel="stylesheet" href="~/css/site.gen.css" asp-append-version="true"/>
```
</details>

_ЁЯз╣ Nitpick_ | _ЁЯФ╡ Trivial_

**Consider adding `Inputs`/`Outputs` for incremental build support.**

The target currently runs on every build. Adding `Inputs` and `Outputs` attributes would enable MSBuild to skip the Tailwind compilation when the input CSS hasn't changed, improving build times.



<details>
<summary>тЩ╗я╕П Proposed enhancement</summary>

```diff
-  <Target Name="RunTailwindBuild" BeforeTargets="BeforeBuild" Condition="'$(TailwindEnabled)' == 'true' AND Exists('$(TailwindInputPath)')">
+  <Target Name="RunTailwindBuild" 
+          BeforeTargets="BeforeBuild" 
+          Condition="'$(TailwindEnabled)' == 'true' AND Exists('$(TailwindInputPath)')"
+          Inputs="$(TailwindInputPath)"
+          Outputs="$(TailwindOutputPath)">
```

Note: For full incremental support, you may need to include additional input files (e.g., `tailwind.config.js`, Razor views) using wildcards.

</details>

_тЪая╕П Potential issue_ | _ЁЯФ┤ Critical_

**Critical: Fallback chain sets a non-existent path without validation, causing CI failures.**

The pipeline failures confirm this issue. The fallback logic on lines 24-25 is broken:

1. Line 24 unconditionally sets `_TailwindToolPath` to `../tailwindcss` when previous conditions fail
2. Line 25's condition `'$(_TailwindToolPath)' == ''` is then always false, so the `.exe` fallback only triggers via the Windows existence check

On Linux CI, the path resolves to a non-existent `../tailwindcss`, causing exit code 127.

Additionally, the PR objectives mention "Automated Tailwind CLI binary management: downloads the Tailwind CLI binary at build-time" but there's no download target in this file. The binary must exist before `RunTailwindBuild` executes.



<details>
<summary>ЁЯРЫ Proposed fix: Add existence validation with actionable error</summary>

```diff
       <!-- Resolve path with fallback chain -->
       <_TailwindToolPath Condition="$([MSBuild]::IsOSPlatform('Windows')) and Exists('$(_TailwindToolPathWin)')">$(_TailwindToolPathWin)</_TailwindToolPath>
       <_TailwindToolPath Condition="'$(_TailwindToolPath)' == '' and Exists('$(_TailwindToolPathNix)')">$(_TailwindToolPathNix)</_TailwindToolPath>
-      <_TailwindToolPath Condition="'$(_TailwindToolPath)' == ''">$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../tailwindcss</_TailwindToolPath>
-      <_TailwindToolPath Condition="'$(_TailwindToolPath)' == '' or (!Exists('$(_TailwindToolPath)') and $([MSBuild]::IsOSPlatform('Windows')))">$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../tailwindcss.exe</_TailwindToolPath>
+      <!-- Local development fallbacks - only set if file exists -->
+      <_TailwindToolPath Condition="'$(_TailwindToolPath)' == '' and Exists('$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../tailwindcss')">$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../tailwindcss</_TailwindToolPath>
+      <_TailwindToolPath Condition="'$(_TailwindToolPath)' == '' and Exists('$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../tailwindcss.exe')">$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../tailwindcss.exe</_TailwindToolPath>
     </PropertyGroup>
 
+    <Error Condition="'$(_TailwindToolPath)' == '' or !Exists('$(_TailwindToolPath)')" 
+           Text="Tailwind CLI not found. Searched: runtimes/$(ComputedRuntimeIdentifier)/native/, $(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../. Run the download target or ensure 'tailwindcss' binary is available." />
+
     <Message Importance="High" Text="Tailwind CSS: Running build for $(TailwindInputPath) -> $(TailwindOutputPath)" />
```

</details>

You'll also need to add a separate target (e.g., `DownloadTailwindCli`) that runs before `RunTailwindBuild` to download and cache the binary. Would you like me to help design that target?

_тЪая╕П Potential issue_ | _ЁЯЯа Major_

**Security: Checksum verification is silently skipped if `ExpectedHash` is empty.**

If the `sha256sums.txt` file doesn't contain an entry for `$(TailwindBinaryName)`, `ExpectedHash` remains empty. The error condition on line 94 requires `'$(ExpectedHash)' != ''`, so an unverified binary will be silently accepted without warning.

Consider adding an error when `ExpectedHash` is empty after running `FindChecksum`:



<details>
<summary>ЁЯЫбя╕П Proposed fix to fail on missing checksum</summary>

```diff
 <FindChecksum ChecksumFile="$(ChecksumFile)" BinaryName="$(TailwindBinaryName)">
   <Output TaskParameter="Hash" PropertyName="ExpectedHash" />
 </FindChecksum>

+<Error Text="Could not find checksum for '$(TailwindBinaryName)' in sha256sums.txt. Verify TailwindBinaryName is correct." Condition="'$(ExpectedHash)' == ''" />
+
 <DownloadFile SourceUrl="$(TailwindDownloadUrl)" DestinationFolder="$(IntermediateOutputPath)" DestinationFileName="tailwindcss_download" Condition="!Exists('$(TailwindLocalPath)')">
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

ExpectedHash is empty (e.g., no entry for $(TailwindBinaryName) in
sha256sums.txt); after the FindChecksum step add a failing Error MSBuild entry
that triggers when ExpectedHash is empty (Condition="'$(ExpectedHash)' == ''")
and emits a clear message referencing $(TailwindBinaryName) so the build fails
if no expected checksum was found; locate where FindChecksum is invoked and
insert this Error check to ensure missing ExpectedHash cannot be silently
accepted.
```

**Maintain synchronization between C#, MSBuild, and targets file RID logic for Windows ARM64 and future platform support.**

`GetCurrentRid()` currently returns `"unknown"` for Windows ARM64, which matches the MSBuild logic in the `.csproj` and `.targets` filesтАФnone define a `win-arm64` case. This is acceptable if Windows ARM64 is not a target platform, but it's important that future updates to platform support stay synchronized across all three locations:

1. `TailwindCliManager.cs` тАФ `GetCurrentRid()` method (lines 66-72)
2. `.csproj` тАФ `ComputedRuntimeIdentifier` properties (lines 23-27)
3. `.targets` тАФ `ComputedRuntimeIdentifier` properties (lines 10-15)

If any of these is updated to support a new RID (e.g., `win-arm64`), all three must be updated in tandem to avoid `FileNotFoundException` at runtime. The graceful fallback to local path and system PATH provides some protection, but the mismatch would still require unnecessary fallback invocation.

Consider adding cross-references in comments between these three locations to prevent accidental drift during future maintenance.

Minor: Line 47 has an extra leading space before `_logger`.

```
MSBuild/.targets RID logic; update GetCurrentRid (and add a clear comment
cross-referencing the matching ComputedRuntimeIdentifier entries in the .csproj
and .targets) so that when you add support for a new RID (e.g., "win-arm64") you
update all three places in tandem; also remove the stray leading space before
```

_ЁЯз╣ Nitpick_ | _ЁЯФ╡ Trivial_

**Consider adding validation for path properties.**

`InputPath` and `OutputPath` are passed directly to the Tailwind CLI by `TailwindWatchService` (see Lines 50-51 in `TailwindWatchService.cs`). If either is set to `null` or empty string, the CLI will receive malformed arguments like `--input  --output  --watch`, causing cryptic failures.

Consider adding runtime validation in `TailwindWatchService` before invoking the CLI, or documenting that these paths must be non-empty.



<details>
<summary>тЩ╗я╕П Example validation in TailwindWatchService.ExecuteAsync</summary>

```csharp
if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_options.InputPath) || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_options.OutputPath))
{
    _logger.LogWarning("Tailwind CSS integration is enabled but InputPath or OutputPath is not configured. Skipping.");
    return;
}
```
</details>

<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
22, TailwindOptions exposes InputPath and OutputPath which can be null/empty and
are passed into TailwindWatchService (ExecuteAsync), causing malformed CLI args;
add validation in TailwindWatchService.ExecuteAsync to check
string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_options.InputPath) ||
string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_options.OutputPath) and bail out (log a warning via
the service logger) or throw a clear exception before building the CLI arguments
so the Tailwind CLI never receives empty --input/--output flags; alternatively,
document that InputPath and OutputPath on TailwindOptions must be non-empty.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>ЁЯзй Analysis chain</summary>

ЁЯМР Web query:

`Does Assembly.Location return empty string for single-file .NET apps?`

ЁЯТб Result:

Yes, Assembly.Location returns an empty string for assemblies in single-file .NET apps (NET 5 and later). This is because bundled assemblies are loaded from memory, not disk-extracted files. Use AppContext.BaseDirectory for the app directory instead.

Citations:

- 1: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/deploying/single-file/warnings/il3000
- 2: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/core-libraries/5.0/assembly-api-behavior-changes-for-single-file-publish
- 3: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/deploying/single-file/overview

---



</details>

**`Assembly.Location` returns empty string for single-file deployments.**

When the application is published as a single-file executable (.NET 5+), `Assembly.Location` returns an empty string because bundled assemblies are loaded from memory rather than disk. This causes `assemblyDir` to be `null` and skips the NuGet runtime path resolution entirely.

Use `AppContext.BaseDirectory` instead, which works correctly for both standard and single-file deployments:

<details>
<summary>тЩ╗я╕П Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-        var assemblyLocation = typeof(TailwindCliManager).Assembly.Location;
-        var assemblyDir = Path.GetDirectoryName(assemblyLocation);
-
-        if (assemblyDir != null)
+        var baseDir = AppContext.BaseDirectory;
+        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(baseDir))
         {
             var rid = GetCurrentRid();
             // Standard NuGet runtime asset path
-            var runtimePath = Path.Combine(assemblyDir, "runtimes", rid, "native", _binaryName);
+            var runtimePath = Path.Combine(baseDir, "runtimes", rid, "native", _binaryName);
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>ЁЯдЦ Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindCliManager.cs` around lines 31 -
52, The code in TailwindCliManager that uses
typeof(TailwindCliManager).Assembly.Location to compute assemblyDir fails for
single-file deployments; replace that logic to use AppContext.BaseDirectory as
the base directory for resolving runtime and local paths (keep using
GetCurrentRid(), _binaryName and the same "runtimes/{rid}/native" and local
filename checks), update the log messages via _logger as before, and ensure the
path existence checks and returns behave identically when using baseDir instead
of assemblyDir.
```

</details>

**`result.Stderr` is always empty when `streamOutput` is true.**

When streaming mode is enabled (line 75), stderr is written directly to the logger and `result.Stderr` returns an empty string. The error message on line 79 will never contain useful stderr content.

<details>
<summary>тЩ╗я╕П Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
             if (result.ExitCode != 0 && !stoppingToken.IsCancellationRequested)
             {
-                _logger.LogError("Tailwind CSS watch process exited with code {ExitCode}. Stderr: {Stderr}", result.ExitCode, result.Stderr);
+                _logger.LogError("Tailwind CSS watch process exited with code {ExitCode}. Check previous log entries for details.", result.ExitCode);
             }
```
</details>
